response.justification: The role demands 8 years of C++ programming for a cloud compute platform, but the candidate's only C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014-2015. Their core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and D-lang, with limited large-scale distributed systems work and no production cloud-compute-platform experience. While they show solid engineering craftsmanship, mentoring inclination, and breadth, the central technical requirement is not met.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with mentoring, workshops, and talks demonstrating leadership instincts
Some distributed-systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling high-throughput microservices
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling
concerns: C++ experience is roughly six months — far short of the 8-year minimum
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership at the scale implied
No evidence of formal technical-lead role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers for 2+ years

key_factors.factor: Cloud compute platform / large-scale infra ownership
